title: Wednesday War Room – 02/25/2026

title: Market & Momentum - 02/23/2026
description: This week opens with sharpened enterprise risk around exploited webmail flaws, remote access takeover paths, and perimeter device compromise... proving (again) that attackers don’t need creativity when your edge is doing the hard work for them.
author: Mycomputerspot Security Newsletter

title: Wednesday War Room – 02/18/2026
description: This Wednesday's threat landscape says it loud: attackers are living off your trust boundaries.

title: Market & Momentum - 02/16/2026
description: This week opens with sharpened enterprise risk around browser zero-days, user-assisted malware staging, and high-volume data exposure, showing (again) that attackers don’t need “advanced” when they can combine speed, scale, and psychology.
author: Mycomputerspot Security Newsletter

title: Wednesday War Room – 02/11/2026
description: This Wednesday's threat landscape is a blunt reminder that attackers do not need “new.” They need unpatched enterprise software, trusted remote-access tooling, and users downloading the wrong thing once.
author: Mycomputerspot Security Newsletter

title: Market & Momentum - 02/09/2026
description: This week opens with sharpened enterprise risk around remote exploitation, data exposure, and strategic espionage campaigns, underscoring how attackers are combining stealth toolkits with fast-moving compromise techniques.
